# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a Laravel service class that sends SMS notifications via Twilio API. The service should handle authentication and message sending. Configure the class to read Twilio credentials from the appropriate configuration source.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

<?php

namespace App\Services;

use Twilio\Rest\Client;
use Exception;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Log;

class TwilioSmsService
{
    protected $client;
    protected $twilioPhoneNumber;

    public function __construct()
    {
        $sid = config('services.twilio.sid');
        $token = config('services.twilio.token');
        $this->twilioPhoneNumber = config('services.twilio.phone_number');

        if (empty($sid) || empty($token) || empty($this->twilioPhoneNumber)) {
            throw new Exception("Twilio credentials are not set in the configuration.");
        }

        $this->client = new Client($sid, $token);
    }

    /**
     * Send an SMS message using Twilio.
     *
     * @param string $to The recipient's phone number (e.g., "+12345678900").
     * @param string $message The content of the SMS message.
     * @return bool True on success, false on failure.
     */
    public function sendSms(string $to, string $message): bool
    {
        try {
            $this->client->messages->create(
                $to,
                [
                    'from' => $this->twilioPhoneNumber,
                    'body' => $message
                ]
            );

            Log::info("SMS sent successfully to {$to} via Twilio.");
            return true;
        } catch (Exception $e) {
            Log::error("Failed to send SMS to {$to} via Twilio: " . $e->getMessage());
            return false;
        }
    }
}
